item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So. It is some wizard that screwed up - teleported to Moon and lost almost all power due to that? Ookay… neutral vote tbh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2686, in its current state, poses no actual threat to the Foundation or its holdings, but steps should be taken to ensure that it does not leave the surface of the moon.

Why is this so funny? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love skips that's exactly humanoids with child-like thoughts and emotions but with adult bodies. It's like a kid trying to fill all the plot-holes in their epic space story. "Well, uh, I'm the Moon Wizard, so hah!"

In all seriousness, I quite enjoy this skip. It's a romp into the mystical and magical and the juxtaposition of the clinical Foundation with the Moon Wizard is entertaining. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Assuming that this interview takes place sometime in the 2000s, the wizard did say that he'd lived on the moon for about 300 years, so the last time he would have heard language would have been sometime in the 1700s. It would sound "old-fashioned", certainly, but it wouldn't be unintelligible.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
So, this exists now, and I hope you enjoy it!

https://youtu.be/LSB25o5KwW0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pan Twardowski lives!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the article was written for a planned moon-based MTF series someone had been working on. They were supposed to be stationed out of that outpost, using the teleporter and whatever else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to SCP-120 the Foundation has a lunar outpost and a teleporter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did we get up there? Do we have a space ship, or does he have a radio, this is bugging me?

Actually now that I think about it, the foundation probably has a fleet lying around somewhere, and if not they could rent one from Russia or something so carry on. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I disagree with the idea that silly ideas should be barred from the main list."

Seconded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yer a wisrd, Arry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…. and the idea of an actual space wizard is now taken. Dammit.

Also, I know I'm being a stick in the mud here, but the article had a beautiful imagery going on there, and then the mage turned out to be a HAM giving quests to astronauts, which would be fine if someone looking for quests actually went there in the first place. Also speaking in ENGLISH. MODERN English.

Did the cat also bring him a radio up there? These past three hundred years must have been boring otherwise.

Anyways, I don't like it too much and find it entirely too silly, buuuuuut it's a cute one and excellently well executed. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, 100% accurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it weird that when the Moon Wizard was shouting, I read his dialogue in Brian Blessed's voice?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted with the force of a thousand moons!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I was going for, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the Foundation observers only noted that there were different kinds of meat, and the wizard told them that one of them was griffin. (Similarly, he also told about the cat that was never observed.) I think it just adds to the wacky personality of SCP-2686 and it's alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "I say, this meat appears to be equal parts lion and eagle, how bizarre!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How does the Foundation know that one of the meats was griffin? Do they know what griffin tastes like? Do they serve griffin burgers in the site cafeterias on alternate Fridays? Have they been hiding those from us, too?!

Also, I disagree with the idea that silly ideas should be barred from the main list. Just because something is silly doesn't mean that it can't be viewed in a serious manner (or prevent it from being a serious threat in the proper circumstances). "Probably memetic or something" is a -J. Just because the researchers might occasionally accost one another with "MOOOOOON WIIIIIZARRRRRD" in the halls doesn't make this a -J if it isn't in the documentation. In documenting it, they are taking it seriously, and the article reflects that.

EDIT: That isn't to say that all silly ideas should be mainlisted (or that no serious ideas should be -Js). Bad ideas are still bad, and bad writing is still bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if he holds a mirror over his head?

+1'd. It was fabulous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey in the third paragraph of the description (on SCP-2686-1) you wrote:

'but this can not be confirmed' (3rd line).

While you can do this I believe a simpler cannot would suffice here, as you are not forming another construction with the not.

For the article itself I felt it was nicely written and concise. Personally though it was a little too jovial for my tastes and I'm not a fan of the wizard idea so a neutral vote.

item.title: Re: Plot hole/Science Fail
item.content:encoded: That almost makes sense. At least, as much sense as a Moon Wizard can make.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is basically what I had in mind.

item.title: Re: Plot hole/Science Fail
item.content:encoded: They pressed a radio right up next to the wall of the perimeter. It was a bit fuzzy, but it worked for the most part. Didn't really think that part needed expanding on, given that none of the other technical aspects of the situation are explained either. I can see what you mean, though.

item.title: Plot hole/Science Fail
item.content:encoded: How did the agent conduct the interview in an atmospheric vacuum?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beyond what's already been stated by Metalton and Goodwill, I don't have much else to say besides "I find it too silly for the mainlist."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My working theory is that he's a refugee from another reality where life is basically exactly like a fantasy rpg.

The cat would be his familiar- a creature or creature-like manifestation of magic soulbound to a mage that does things like run errands and aids in the focusing of magic. So not so much space cat as magic cat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of some powerful supernatural being accidentally fucking itself over for a laughably ridiculous reason. However, I don't like the idea that in the Foundation's universe, fantasy role-playing games are pretty much just historical re-enactments. I suppose this guy isn't necessarily telling the truth, but I can't really infer any substantial alternative explanation from the article alone. No-vote.

Also, what the fuck is up with the space cat? Even with the wizard being as silly as he is, that just doesn't seem to fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny, but the interview was a little too on the nose for me. It felt too much like a Monty Python sketch for me to see it as a mainlister. Neutral vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hopefully, Djoric will write it right after he writes the catgirls stealing the Foundation's spaceship. Also, I have to point out that they're cyborg catgirls.

Actually, a full-length description of them would be Japanese cyborg catgirl warriors… and a space wizard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone must write this crossover…

item.title: Loved it.
item.content:encoded:
My only problem is that I found the interviewers tone rather casual for someone who is apparently both a Foundation agent and an astronaut but overall it added to the kind of whimsical tone of the whole thing.

Also, I assume the experiment of giving this guy a ride back to Earth to see what happens is pending approval.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2686: And we are on the moon itself, after all.
████████: I see.
SCP-2686: Where the moonlight is rather [I]below[/I].

… Take my upvote, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should be Keter. After all, who knows what will happen if he ever gets the idea to stand on his head?

Joking, of course. I like this one, it's just funny, and a nice departure from the tone of most skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall, I found this amusing and charming. That said, while I upvoted, my only thought was "Don't we already have a space wizard? And he comes with four cats!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MOOOON WIIZARD!

I refuse to believe that the Foundation Officials don't shout that at every given opportunity.

MOOOOOOOOOOOOOON WIIZZZZZAAAAAAAAAAARRD!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't like explicitly calling it a wizard (even indirectly) in the formal description. (For one, where did they get Welsh from?) But dammit, everything else about it is pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea. I especially loved the log at the end.
Well done.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope. I have no idea what you're talking about.

This is what I was talking about.
Edit: And now it's gone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I remember what I pay you people for.

Brilliant. Just brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could be that you're thinking of that Destiny meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I could've sworn there was talk of a poorly-written moon wizard earlier today… Anyways, I enjoyed the SCP, but now all I can think is, "When will this guy, the Black Rabbit Company, and that one other wizard meet up and duke it out?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it isn't a rewrite. The idea came from Roget telling me in the GM room for the Origins RP that he wanted to do some SCP tales set on the moon base, and the fact that I was writing about wizards for the RP at the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rated it, and it went from +3 to +10. I must be a goddamn moon wizard!

This is funny. I take it this is a rewrite of something deleted recently?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s an SCP about a moon wizard on the moon.

Thanks to Zyn, Tahunu, TwistedGears, Faminepulse, djkaktus, Dmatix, Smapti, Djoric, Tuomey, Light, Dr_Leonard, Soulless, and Vezaz for looking it over in chat.

Let me know if you find any errors or areas that need improvement.
